title: Cross-chain test vectors for conformance suite

artifacts.description: JSON Schema defining the structure of each cross-chain test vector: sourceChainId, targetChainId, relayTimestamp, rootAge, proof, expectedResult, failureReason
Vector: proof generated on chain A (chainId=1) submitted to verifier on chain B (chainId=137) without chain ID binding — expected FAIL
Vector: proof generated and verified on same logical chain with correct chain ID binding — expected PASS
Vector: Merkle root relayed with age < maxRootAge threshold — expected PASS
Vector: Merkle root relayed with age > maxRootAge threshold (relay delay simulation) — expected FAIL
Vector: storage proof with a known-valid block hash on the source chain — expected PASS
Vector: storage proof with a tampered or non-existent block hash — expected FAIL
Vector: storage proof with block hash from a different chain than declared sourceChainId — expected FAIL
Vector: batch checkpoint inclusion proof where leaf is correctly included in the batch Merkle tree — expected PASS
Vector: batch checkpoint inclusion proof with incorrect leaf value — expected FAIL
Vector: batch checkpoint proof with proof path valid but root mismatched — expected FAIL
Vector: nullifier reuse attempt on the same chain — expected FAIL
Vector: nullifier from chain A replayed on chain B where nullifier set is not cross-chain-scoped — expected FAIL
Vector: distinct nullifier used on chain B after valid use on chain A with chain-scoped nullifier set — expected PASS
Vector: relayTimestamp set in the future beyond block.timestamp tolerance — expected FAIL
Vector: relayTimestamp field absent from proof public inputs — expected FAIL (schema violation)
Human-readable specification describing each vector's intent, the protocol invariant it tests, and instructions for bridge implementers to run the suite
TypeScript test harness that loads all cv_*.json vectors, submits them to a configurable verifier contract or off-chain verifier function, and asserts expectedResult matches actual outcome

Minimal Solidity verifier stub implementing chain ID binding, root age check, storage proof verification, batch inclusion, and nullifier set — used as reference implementation for running vectors on-chain
Guide for third-party bridge implementers: how to plug their verifier into the runner, expected pass/fail counts, and how to add custom vectors following the schema

steps: Define and publish conformance/cross_chain_vector_schema.json with required fields: sourceChainId (uint64), targetChainId (uint64), relayTimestamp (unix seconds), rootAge (seconds), proof (hex), publicInputs (array), expectedResult (pass|fail), failureReason (string|null)

Implement MockCrossChainVerifier.sol with five independently toggleable invariant checks: chain ID binding, root age TTL, storage proof block hash verification, batch Merkle inclusion, and scoped nullifier set

Author cv_01 and cv_02 (chain ID binding vectors): generate a real Groth16 or Plonk proof with sourceChainId in public inputs, craft the bound and unbound variants, record raw proof bytes and expected results
Author cv_03 and cv_04 (stale root vectors): parameterize rootAge field, set maxRootAge = 3600s in mock verifier, produce two vectors straddling the boundary

Author cv_05, cv_06, and cv_07 (storage proof vectors): use a known Ethereum state trie snapshot for valid block hash; mutate one byte for the invalid variant; use a Polygon block hash against an Ethereum-declared sourceChainId for the wrong-chain variant
Author cv_08, cv_09, and cv_10 (batch checkpoint vectors): construct a small Merkle tree of 8 leaves, produce valid inclusion proof for cv_08, corrupt leaf value for cv_09, swap root for cv_10

Author cv_11, cv_12, and cv_13 (nullifier replay vectors): define a nullifier as keccak256(chainId || identity || nonce); replay same value on same chain for cv_11; replay on different chain against non-scoped set for cv_12; use distinct values with chain-scoped sets for cv_13
Author cv_14 and cv_15 (relay timestamp edge cases): set relayTimestamp = block.timestamp + 3600 for cv_14; omit field entirely and expect schema validation failure for cv_15

Implement conformance/runner/cross_chain_runner.ts: parse all cv_*.json files, instantiate MockCrossChainVerifier via ethers.js against a local Hardhat/Anvil node, call verify() for each vector, compare result to expectedResult, print per-vector pass/fail and aggregate summary
Write CROSS_CHAIN_VECTORS.md documenting each vector's invariant, the specific protocol rule it exercises, and the canonical failure mode a broken implementation would exhibit

Write IMPLEMENTER_GUIDE.md: how to substitute a custom verifier address in the runner, minimum interface the verifier must expose (verify(proof, publicInputs) → bool), how to extend the suite with new vectors
Add a CI job (GitHub Actions) that spins up Anvil, deploys MockCrossChainVerifier, runs cross_chain_runner.ts, and asserts zero unexpected failures — gate merges on this job
Tag conformance suite as v1.0.0 and publish artifact checksums in CROSS_CHAIN_VECTORS.md so implementers can verify vector integrity
dependencies: Groth16 or Plonk proof system library (snarkjs or bellman) for generating real proof bytes in vectors
Ethereum state trie snapshot or mock MPT library for storage proof vectors
Hardhat or Foundry + Anvil for local EVM execution in CI
ethers.js or viem for TypeScript runner contract interaction
Existing conformance test suite scaffold (if any) to integrate cross-chain vectors alongside existing unit vectors
Defined maxRootAge and relay TTL constants from the protocol specification
Nullifier scoping scheme (chain-scoped vs global) finalized in protocol spec before authoring cv_11–cv_13
